study_info.title: The Clinical Spectrum of Multiple Endocrine Neoplasia Type 2a Caused by the Rare Intracellular RET Mutation S891A
study_info.journal: The Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism
study_info.study_type: Retrospective multicenter case series with literature review
institutions: Department of Endocrine Surgery, King’s College Hospital, King’s Health Partners, London SE5 9RS, United Kingdom
Department of Endocrinology, King’s College Hospital, King’s Health Partners, London SE5 9RS, United Kingdom
Department of Pathology, King’s College Hospital, King’s Health Partners, London SE5 9RS, United Kingdom
Department of Genetics, Guy’s and St. Thomas’ Hospital, King’s Health Partners, London SE5 9RS, United Kingdom
Dipartimento di Scienze Mediche, Universita` degli Studi di Milano, Milan, Italy
Endocrine Unit, Fondazione Istituto di Ricovero e Cura a Carattere Scientifico Ca’ Granda, I-20122 Milan, Italy
Department of Surgery, Martin-Luther-University of Halle, D-06108 Halle, Germany

cohort_info.description: Combined cohort of 36 RET S891A carriers from three centers and 38 additional carriers from nine published studies (total 74 carriers).
centers: London (King’s College Hospital / Guy’s and St. Thomas’ Hospital)
Milan (Universita` degli Studi di Milano / Fondazione IRCCS Ca’ Granda)

variants.clinical_classification: American Thyroid Association class A mutation (low-risk category for MEN2-associated RET variants)
variants.transforming_activity_comment: Reported as a RET mutation with low transforming activity compared with high-activity mutations such as A883F and M918T.

patients.family_history: MEN2A family with RET S891A mutation (exact pedigree details not provided)

surgeries.type: total thyroidectomy
surgeries.extent: total thyroidectomy with central neck dissection level VI

patients.family_history: Index case of family; later family screening revealed additional carriers (including KMS-10 as mother).
patients.presenting_symptoms: Severe arterial hypertension, spells, headaches due to pheochromocytoma.

imaging.pheochromocytoma_CT: Pheochromocytoma identified by computed tomography (right/left side not specified).

pheochromocytoma_details.local_invasion: locally invasive; extension through adrenal capsule in multiple areas; infiltration of periadrenal soft tissue
pheochromocytoma_details.pattern: nested pattern of tumor cells with basophilic granular cytoplasm
pheochromocytoma_details.mitotic_rate: fewer than one mitotic figure per 50 high-power fields

surgeries.type: laparoscopic adrenalectomy plus thyroid and neck surgery
surgeries.extent: Laparoscopic adrenalectomy for pheochromocytoma; total thyroidectomy and bilateral modified radical neck dissection (MRND) levels II–V.

surgeries.type: redo bilateral MRND
surgeries.extent: Redo modified radical bilateral neck dissection (levels II–V bilaterally).
surgeries.timing_relative_to_diagnosis: after detection of persistent calcitonin (exact interval not reported)

staging.pN: N1a (initial 1/62 lymph nodes positive; counting distribution: 1/7 central and 0/55 lateral reported in table)

follow_up_status.persistent_MTC: Yes, initially; biochemical cure after redo surgery
follow_up_status.calcitonin_persistence_or_recurrence: Persistent calcitonin after first operation; undetectable after redo MRND.
follow_up_status.follow_up_duration_months: Not precisely reported; mother’s follow-up noted separately.
follow_up_status.outcome: alive and biochemically cured of MTC and pheochromocytoma at last follow-up
follow_up_status.comments: Mother (KMS-10) died from locoregional MTC recurrence after multiple prior surgeries in another hospital.

patients.family_history: Member of RET S891A kindred; detailed pedigree not given.

RET_S891A.intracellular_mutation_fraction_of_all_R: up to 5% of all RET mutation carriers and 16% of those with intracellular mutations
RET_S891A.persistence_of_MTC_in_patients_with_foll: 14.3% (text statement) and 37.9% among 29 surgically treated in case series

methods.patient_identification: RET S891A carriers (n=36) were identified from existing databases in London, Halle, and Milan centers.
methods.DNA_source: Affected or at-risk family members after genetic counseling and informed consent (biological sample type not specified).
methods.sequencing_methods: Sequence analysis of RET exons 10, 11, 13, 14, 15, and 16 in accredited genetic testing laboratories (specific platform not stated).

clinical_evaluation.calcitonin_assays: Basal calcitonin measured in all patients.
clinical_evaluation.thyroid_imaging: Ultrasound in 33 of 35 patients; fine-needle aspiration, 99mTc scintiscan or dimercaptosuccinic acid scan as indicated.
clinical_evaluation.pheochromocytoma_workup: Triple 24-h urine collection for catecholamines; specific imaging (CT, MIBG) in 8 of 35 patients.
clinical_evaluation.parathyroid_workup: Serum calcium and PTH assessed; PH defined as raised corrected serum calcium and PTH.

pentagastrin_test_protocol.Milan: Pentagastrin 0.5 µg/kg iv bolus; calcitonin measured at -10, 0, 2, 5, and 10 minutes.
pentagastrin_test_protocol.Halle: Pentagastrin 0.5 µg/kg iv bolus; calcitonin measured at 0, 2, and 5 minutes.

histopathology.WHO_classification: Tumors classified according to WHO classification of tumours of endocrine organs (2004).
histopathology.C_cell_hyperplasia_definition: At least 50 C cells in one low-power field (100x).
histopathology.TNM_staging: TNM for medullary and papillary thyroid cancer according to UICC 6th edition 2006.
histopathology.pheochromocytoma_definition: Histologic tumor of chromaffin cells of adrenal medulla ≥1 cm in diameter.
histopathology.parathyroid_hyperplasia_definition: Glands >60 mg with diffuse or nodular growth, variable adipose tissue, mild nuclear pleomorphism, absence of normal/atrophic parenchyma, with internodular transition if nodular.

methods.statistical_tests: Not explicitly named; descriptive statistics (means, percentages, age-related penetrance) are reported.

lymph_node_metastasis_pattern.surgical_variation: Extent of lymph node dissection varied between centers.
lymph_node_metastasis_pattern.London_neck_dissection: 6 of 10 London patients had ipsilateral MRND II–V; 4 had additional contralateral MRND.
lymph_node_metastasis_pattern.Halle_neck_dissection: 3 of 8 Halle patients had bilateral MRND due to high calcitonin or macroscopic cancer.

persistence_and_recurrence.redo_surgery_outcome_KMS_2: One additional metastatic lymph node found among 111 resected; calcitonin normalized (biochemical cure).
persistence_and_recurrence.death_from_MTC: One patient (KMS-10) died from locoregional MTC recurrence at age 69 after multiple neck surgeries.

pheochromocytoma_results.index_PHEO_case_description: KMS-2 presented with spells, headaches, severe hypertension; had markedly elevated catecholamines and locally invasive adrenal pheochromocytoma removed laparoscopically.
pheochromocytoma_results.pheochromocytoma_invasion_features: Capsular transgression, periadrenal soft tissue infiltration, low mitotic rate, low Ki-67, no necrosis or vascular/perineural invasion.

parathyroid_results.borderline_calcium_patient: 1 patient with 2.6 mM (upper limit 2.6 mM), normal PTH.
parathyroid_results.PH_clinical_comment: Histologic PH present without biochemical hyperparathyroidism; PH considered of little functional consequence.

papillary_thyroid_cancer_results.PTC_histology: Papillary thyroid microcarcinoma, sometimes multifocal, without nodal metastases.
papillary_thyroid_cancer_results.epidemiologic_comment: PTC rate close to background; argued to be coincidental rather than causally related to RET S891A.

corneal_nerve_thickening_results.CNT_detection: Detected by slit lamp examination; moderate CNT likely underreported.
corneal_nerve_thickening_results.CNT_context: Previously thought exclusive to MEN2B; now recognized in >60% of MEN2A in general and in a minority of intracellular RET mutations including S891A.

age_related_penetrance.text_summary: By age >60 years, MTC penetrance in RET S891A carriers approaches 100%.

extra_regional_metastases_and_other_canc.colon_cancer_case_series: One colon cancer among case series patients (Fug-III/8).

functional_data.comparative_strength_vs_known_mutants: S891A is classified as a low-transforming-activity RET mutation compared with high-activity mutations like A883F and M918T, based on prior functional work (Iwashita et al.).

Table_1.title_or_caption: RET S891A mutation: clinical presentation, treatment, and outcome of 36 patients with RET S891A mutation (individual data).
Table_1.description: Individual-level data for 36 S891A carriers including age at diagnosis, gender, preoperative calcitonin (times upper normal limit), pentagastrin test, CCH, MTC, persistent MTC, total thyroidectomy, central and lateral lymph node status, pT and pN stage, papillary thyroid cancer, parathyroid pathology, pheochromocytoma.
Table_1.numeric_values_note: Numeric entries include ages, calcitonin multiples of normal, lymph node counts (positive/total), tumor stages; all patient-level entries are captured in the patients array where possible.

Table_2.title_or_caption: Clinical presentation and treatment in 74 patients with RET S891A mutation.
Table_2.description: Clustered summary across current 36-patient case series and nine prior studies, reporting totals and percentages of affected individuals, asymptomatic carriers, mean age at diagnosis, rates of thyroidectomy, central/lateral lymph node dissection, MTC, CCH, PTC, PH, PHEO, CNT, extra-regional metastases, and other cancers.
Table_2.numeric_values_note: Numeric values include numbers and percentages per study; key totals and percentages have been extracted into mutation_summary and results sections.

Table_3.title_or_caption: Age-related penetrance of 36 patients with RET S891A mutation.
Table_3.description: Shows number of cancer patients, gene carriers, and calculated penetrance (%) in age ranges 0–20, 21–40, 41–60, and >60 years.
Table_3.numeric_values_note: Penetrance values 20.0, 71.4, 92.3, 100.0 are captured in results.age_related_penetrance.

discussion_notes: RET S891A is an intracellular TK2 domain mutation with low transforming activity compared with high-risk mutations such as A883F and M918T.
Despite lower transforming activity, S891A can produce the full MEN2A phenotype including medullary thyroid carcinoma, pheochromocytoma, and corneal nerve thickening.
S891A accounts for up to 5% of all reported RET mutation carriers and 16% of those with intracellular mutations.
S891A has been detected in 2.1% of German RET families and 4.8% of Continental European RET families.
Medullary thyroid carcinoma penetrance in S891A carriers is high, reaching about 100% by older age categories.
The youngest age at onset for medullary thyroid carcinoma in S891A carriers was 17 years, indicating early potential onset.

Several S891A carriers show medullary thyroid carcinoma without background C-cell hyperplasia, suggesting a different tumorigenic pathway than that of extracellular cysteine mutations where CCH precedes MTC.
The absence of lymph node metastasis in most S891A patients with medullary thyroid carcinoma suggests a mitigated propensity for lymphatic spread compared with high-risk RET mutations.
The American Thyroid Association classifies RET S891A as a class A mutation, and its guidelines for this risk group should be followed.
Given the moderate metastatic potential of S891A-related medullary thyroid carcinoma, secondary surgical cure is feasible in some patients with persistent calcitonin after initial surgery.
Papillary thyroid carcinoma co-occurrence in S891A carriers appears close to background population rates and is likely coincidental rather than causally linked.
S891A-related pheochromocytoma is rare but documented, and pheochromocytoma must be an integral part of clinical screening in S891A carriers.
Parathyroid hyperplasia in S891A carriers is usually subclinical with normal calcium and PTH levels and appears of little functional consequence.
Corneal nerve thickening, once thought exclusive to MEN2B, can occur in MEN2A including S891A and may be underrecognized without slit lamp examination.
Undertreatment of S891A-related medullary thyroid carcinoma may lead to recurrence and death, underscoring the need for adherence to MEN2A management guidelines.
The study supports that intracellular RET mutations, including S891A, can have a broader MEN2A spectrum than previously assumed from early reports associating them mainly with familial MTC.
